goal: Implement 6 UI/UX research-backed decisions from docs/ui-ux-decisions.md: (1) Semantic colors for status components with WCAG AA-validated palette, (2) Progressive disclosure on My Week page, (3) AI suggestion tiering to reduce fatigue, (4) Dual font stack (Inter + Geist Mono) via host-token pipeline, (5) Charts library migration to Tremor/Recharts, (6) Loading & empty state improvements. Changes are scoped to the frontend package and the shared DesignTokens contract.

steps.title: Add WCAG AA-validated semantic color tokens to CSS theme
steps.description:
Decision 1, Part 1: Define semantic color tokens in `frontend/src/index.css`.

The gap analysis found that several proposed palette values fail WCAG AA contrast for normal text (4.5:1 minimum). Before implementing, use corrected values that pass contrast checks:

**Light mode (on white-ish backgrounds):**
- Success base: `#3D7A4A` (was `#5F7A66`, boosted saturation/darkness for ÔëÑ4.5:1 on `#EEF3EF`)
- Warning base: `#7A6520` (was `#8A7A5A`, darkened for ÔëÑ4.5:1 on `#F5F2EA`)
- Danger base: `#9B3B3B` (was `#8A5C5C`, increased contrast for ÔëÑ4.5:1 on `#F6EEEE`)
- Info/AI base: `#4A5578` (was `#5F6B85`, darkened for ÔëÑ4.5:1 on `#EEF1F6`)

**Dark mode (on dark backgrounds):**
- Success base: `#8FBF9A` (was `#7E9B87`, lightened for ÔëÑ4.5:1 on `#1A221C`)
- Warning base: `#C4AC6A` (was `#A39473`, lightened for ÔëÑ4.5:1 on `#211F18`)
- Danger base: `#D4A0A0` (was `#A37878`, lightened for ÔëÑ4.5:1 on `#221A1A`)
- Info/AI base: `#9DABC8` (was `#7C8AA6`, lightened for ÔëÑ4.5:1 on `#1A1D24`)

Background and border values from the original plan are fine (they don't carry text).

Add new CSS custom properties in the `@theme` block:
- `--color-success`, `--color-success-bg`, `--color-success-border`
- `--color-warning`, `--color-warning-bg`, `--color-warning-border`
- `--color-danger`, `--color-danger-bg`, `--color-danger-border`
- `--color-info`, `--color-info-bg`, `--color-info-border`

Update the existing `--color-success`, `--color-warning`, `--color-danger` values (which currently are gray `#404040`/`#737373`/`#171717` in light, vivid in dark) to the new muted semantic values. Add new `-bg` and `-border` tokens.

Also update the `[data-theme="dark"]` block accordingly. Keep `--color-success-foreground`, `--color-warning-foreground`, `--color-danger-foreground` valid for button use (ensure they contrast against the base colors).

Update `MockHostProvider.tsx` `defaultDesignTokens` to match the new light-mode success/warning/danger values.

Update `AppShell.tsx` `tokensToStyle` to pass through the new token values.

Note: The `DesignTokens` interface in `packages/shared/src/host.ts` already has `colorSuccess`, `colorWarning`, `colorDanger` ÔÇö these continue to work. The new `-bg` and `-border` tokens are CSS-only (not part of the host contract) since they are implementation details of the module's internal design system.

steps.title: Update Badge, Toast, and Button components for semantic colors
steps.description:
Decision 1, Part 2: Update status components to use the new semantic color tokens instead of monochrome foreground-based styling.

**Badge.tsx** ÔÇö Replace the `success`, `warning`, `danger` variant classes:
- `success`: `border-success-border bg-success-bg text-success` (was `border-foreground/25 bg-foreground/5 text-foreground`)
- `warning`: `border-warning-border bg-warning-bg text-warning` (was `border-foreground/15 bg-foreground/[0.03] text-muted`)
- `danger`: `border-danger-border bg-danger-bg text-danger font-bold` (was `border-foreground/30 bg-foreground/8 text-foreground font-bold`)
- Add new `info` variant: `border-info-border bg-info-bg text-info`

Keep plan-state badge variants (`draft`, `locked`, `reconciling`, `reconciled`) monochrome.

**Toast.tsx** ÔÇö Update `toastVariants` to use semantic tokens:
- `success`: `border-success-border bg-success-bg text-success`
- `warning`: `border-warning-border bg-warning-bg text-warning`
- `error`: `border-danger-border bg-danger-bg text-danger`
- `info`: `border-info-border bg-info-bg text-info`

**Button.tsx** ÔÇö Update `danger` and `success` button variants to use the new semantic base colors. The `danger` variant: `bg-danger text-white` (ensure white contrasts against the new danger base). The `success` variant: `bg-success text-white`.

Register the new color tokens in `@theme` so Tailwind utilities like `text-success`, `bg-success-bg`, `border-success-border` etc. resolve properly.

Update `frontend/src/__tests__/ui/Badge.test.tsx` assertions ÔÇö tests currently check for `text-foreground` and `text-muted` on semantic variants; update to check for `text-success`, `text-warning`, `text-danger` respectively.

steps.title: Apply semantic colors to status indicators across the app
steps.description:
Decision 1, Part 3: Update all status-indicating components to use semantic colors with icon + text label redundancy.

**MyWeek.tsx ÔÇö ComplianceBadge**: Already uses `text-success`/`text-warning` ÔÇö will now resolve to the new muted semantic colors automatically. Verify the Ô£ô/ÔÜá icon + text pattern is preserved.

**MyWeek.tsx ÔÇö PlanStateBadge**: Keep monochrome (plan states are not severity indicators).

**Reports.tsx ÔÇö ExceptionAgingTable**: Update `SEVERITY_STYLES` map:
- `CRITICAL`: use danger semantic tokens
- `HIGH`: use warning semantic tokens
- `MEDIUM`: keep neutral
- `LOW`: keep neutral
Update the STALE/AGING/NEW status labels to use `text-danger`/`text-warning`/`text-muted` (already correct).

**Reports.tsx ÔÇö VelocityTrendChart**: Trend indicator already uses `text-success`/`text-danger`.

**Reports.tsx ÔÇö ComplianceChart**: Update Shield icon to use `text-success` (already correct).

**InsightPanel.tsx ÔÇö severityBadgeVariant**: Already maps HIGHÔåÆdanger, MEDIUMÔåÆwarning ÔÇö now those Badge variants will render with semantic colors.

**ProactiveRiskBanner.tsx**: Update `SIGNAL_CONFIG` classes from `border-neutral-400 bg-neutral-100` to use danger/warning semantic background tokens for severity differentiation.

**RiskSignalsPanel.tsx**: Update `SIGNAL_CLS` from neutral grays to semantic tokens based on signal severity.

**ScopeChangeDialog.tsx**: Already uses `text-success`/`text-danger` for action type labels.

**ReconcileAssistPanel.tsx**: Update `OUTCOME_COLORS` to use semantic tokens for ACHIEVED (success), NOT_ACHIEVED (danger), PARTIALLY_ACHIEVED (warning).

**TicketDetailView.tsx**: Already maps outcomes to `text-success`/`text-warning`/`text-danger`.

Ensure every status color use has icon + text label for accessibility redundancy (not color alone).

steps.title: Install Inter font and update font token pipeline for dual font stack
steps.description:
Decision 4, Part 1: Switch the base font from monospace to proportional sans-serif.

The font pipeline flows through 3 layers: (1) `packages/shared/src/host.ts` DesignTokens contract, (2) `frontend/src/host/MockHostProvider.tsx` default values, (3) `frontend/src/components/AppShell.tsx` which sets `--font-family-base` as an inline CSS var, and (4) `frontend/src/index.css` which uses it in `@theme` and `body`.

**Changes:**

1. **`frontend/src/index.css`**: Add Google Fonts import for Inter alongside Geist Mono:
   `@import url("https://fonts.googleapis.com/css2?family=Inter:wght@300..900&family=Geist+Mono:wght@300..900&display=swap");`
   Update `@theme` `--font-family-base` default to: `var(--font-family-base, "Inter", "Geist Sans", system-ui, sans-serif)`
   Keep `--font-family-mono` as-is.

2. **`frontend/src/host/MockHostProvider.tsx`**: Update `defaultDesignTokens.fontFamilyBase` from the monospace string to `"'Inter', 'Geist Sans', system-ui, sans-serif"`.

3. **`frontend/src/components/AppShell.tsx`**: No structural changes needed ÔÇö `tokensToStyle` already passes `tokens.fontFamilyBase` through to `--font-family-base`. The inline `fontFamily: 'var(--font-family-base)'` on the root div continues to work.

4. **Note on `packages/shared/src/host.ts`**: The `DesignTokens.fontFamilyBase` field is a string ÔÇö no type change needed. The contract is that the host provides a font family string; we're just changing the default.

This step only changes the default font. The next step handles adding `font-mono` classes to preserve monospace where needed.

steps.title: Add font-mono utility classes to code-like content
steps.description:
Decision 4, Part 2: Now that the base font is proportional, explicitly apply `font-mono` (which maps to `--font-family-mono` = Geist Mono) to code-like content that should remain monospace.

Audit and update these components:

**Already using `font-mono`** (no change needed):
- `TicketListView.tsx` ÔÇö ticket keys
- `TicketDetailView.tsx` ÔÇö ticket keys
- `UncommittedWorkSection.tsx` ÔÇö ticket keys
- `WhatIfPanel.tsx` ÔÇö IDs

**Need `font-mono` added:**
- `CommitList.tsx` ÔÇö commit titles (add `font-mono` to the title text span)
- `CommitForm.tsx` ÔÇö the title input field (add `font-mono` class)
- `CapacityMeter.tsx` ÔÇö estimate point values (the tally `{totalPoints} / {budgetPoints} pts`)
- `Reports.tsx` ÔÇö data cells in charts (point values, percentages, date labels marked as `text-[9px]` or `text-[10px]`)
- `ScopeChangeTimeline.tsx` ÔÇö commit titles in timeline events
- `RcdoTreeView.tsx` ÔÇö RCDO path identifiers
- `AiLintPanel.tsx` ÔÇö no change (prose suggestions should be sans-serif)
- `Header.tsx` ÔÇö week date display (date/time values)

**Should NOT get font-mono** (keep proportional):
- Navigation labels
- Page headings (h1, h2)
- Card titles
- Button text
- Body text / descriptions
- Form labels
- AI insight text
- AI summary prose

Update related tests if any assert on class names that would change.

steps.title: Create CollapsibleSection shared component with localStorage persistence
steps.description:
Decision 2, Prerequisite: Build a reusable `CollapsibleSection` component before refactoring My Week.

Create `frontend/src/components/shared/CollapsibleSection.tsx`:

```tsx
interface CollapsibleSectionProps {
  id: string;               // localStorage key suffix
  title: string;            // Section heading
  defaultExpanded?: boolean;
  badge?: ReactNode;        // e.g., "3 suggestions" count badge
  children: ReactNode;
}
```

Features:
- Renders a header row with title, optional badge, and chevron toggle
- Persists expand/collapse state to `localStorage` under key `wc-section-{id}`
- Supports `defaultExpanded` prop (used on first visit before localStorage has a value)
- Accessible: uses `aria-expanded`, `aria-controls`, and `role="region"` on content
- Styled consistently with the existing Card/CardHeader pattern (border-border, bg-surface)
- Smooth height transition using CSS `grid-template-rows` trick (0fr ÔåÆ 1fr)

Also create `frontend/src/components/shared/usePersistedState.ts` ÔÇö a generic hook for localStorage-backed state used by CollapsibleSection and potentially other components.

Write unit tests in `frontend/src/__tests__/CollapsibleSection.test.tsx`:
- Renders title and children
- Toggles open/close on click
- Respects defaultExpanded prop
- Persists state to localStorage
- Renders badge when provided

steps.title: Implement progressive disclosure on My Week page
steps.description:
Decision 2: Refactor `frontend/src/routes/MyWeek.tsx` to wrap sections in CollapsibleSection and add conditional visibility.

**Always visible** (no change):
- Plan header with state badge + action buttons
- Capacity meter
- Commit list
- Week selector + page header

**Contextually visible** (add conditional guards ÔÇö some already exist):
- Risk banners: already guarded by `isLocked` Ô£ô
- Auto-lock system banner: already guarded by `plan?.systemLockedWithErrors` Ô£ô
- Scope change timeline: already guarded by `isLocked && scopeChangeEvents.length > 0` Ô£ô
- Pre-lock validation: already guarded by `isDraft && showPreLockValidation` Ô£ô

**Wrap in CollapsibleSection** (with defaults):
- AI Lint Panel: `<CollapsibleSection id="ai-lint" title="AI Quality Check" defaultExpanded={false} badge={<Badge>...</Badge>}>`. Extract lint result count to show as badge summary (e.g., "3 hints").
- AI Insights (InsightPanel): `<CollapsibleSection id="ai-insights" title="AI Insights" defaultExpanded={false}>`
- What-If Planner: Already collapsed via its own UI Ô£ô ÔÇö but wrap in CollapsibleSection for consistency
- Plan History: Already has show/hide toggle ÔÇö replace with CollapsibleSection for consistency
- Carry-forward Lineage: Keep as-is (it's triggered by specific commit action, not always present)
- Soft Warnings Panel: `<CollapsibleSection id="soft-warnings" title="Warnings" defaultExpanded={shouldExpandWarnings}>` where `shouldExpandWarnings` is true when commits > 8 or pawn ratio > 40%

**Add "Expand all / Collapse all" toggle** at the top of the page for power users. Implement as a button that programmatically sets all section localStorage keys.

Preserve all existing `data-testid` attributes and ensure existing tests still pass.

steps.title: Implement AI suggestion tiering and dismiss memory
steps.description:
Decision 3: Adjust AI component default states and add dismiss memory.

**Changes to AI components:**

1. **AiLintPanel** (already addressed in step-7 via CollapsibleSection with collapsed default + badge summary). No additional changes needed beyond the collapse wrapper.

2. **InsightPanel** on My Week: Already addressed in step-7 (collapsed by default with CollapsibleSection).

3. **ManagerAiSummaryCard** on Team Week (`frontend/src/routes/TeamWeek.tsx`): Wrap in CollapsibleSection with `id="manager-ai-summary"` and `defaultExpanded={false}`. Show the first line of `summaryText` as a preview in the section header badge.

4. **ProactiveRiskBanner** on My Week: Add a count badge approach ÔÇö show a summary line "ÔÜá N risk signals detected" that expands to full banners on click. Wrap in CollapsibleSection with `id="risk-banners"` and `defaultExpanded={true}` (critical signals should default open but can be collapsed).

5. **Reconcile page AI prefill** (`frontend/src/routes/Reconcile.tsx`): The live AI prefill flow is implemented directly in `Reconcile.tsx` via `useAutoReconcileAssist`, `aiSuggestedOutcomes` state, and a `useEffect` that auto-applies outcomes into `localOutcomes`. Change this from auto-apply to ghost/preview state:
   - Instead of immediately setting `localOutcomes` with AI values in the `useEffect`, store AI suggestions only in `aiSuggestedOutcomes` (do NOT merge into `localOutcomes` until user confirms).
   - In `ReconcileCommitRow`, when a commit has an entry in `aiSuggestedOutcomes` but NOT in `localOutcomes`, render the `OutcomeSelector` in a ghost/preview visual state: faded text with `bg-info-bg` background, the `AiSuggestedBadge` prominently shown, and a "Click to accept" affordance.
   - When the user clicks the ghost outcome or explicitly selects any outcome, move it from `aiSuggestedOutcomes` into `localOutcomes` (solidifying the selection).
   - Similarly for AI-prefilled notes: show them as placeholder/ghost text in the textarea (using `placeholder` or a faded overlay) rather than pre-filling `localNotes`.
   - The `ReconcileAssistPanel` component (`frontend/src/components/ai/ReconcileAssistPanel.tsx`) is a separate manual-trigger panel not currently used by the Reconcile page ÔÇö leave it unchanged in this step.

6. **TeamWeek InsightPanel**: Already has a toggle (`insightsExpanded`). Change default to collapsed: `const [insightsExpanded, setInsightsExpanded] = useState(false);`

**Dismiss memory system:**
Create `frontend/src/lib/useDismissMemory.ts` hook:
- Tracks dismiss count per component ID in localStorage
- `useDismissMemory(componentId: string, threshold?: number)` returns `{ shouldAutoCollapse: boolean, recordDismiss: () => void }`
- Default threshold: 3 consecutive dismissals ÔåÆ auto-collapse
- Wire into AiLintPanel and InsightPanel CollapsibleSection defaults

**Global AI preference:**
Add to the existing feature flags or create a new localStorage-based setting `wc-ai-mode` with values `'full'` | `'on-demand'`. When `'on-demand'`, all AI sections default to collapsed and auto-run is disabled. Add a small toggle in the sidebar footer or header settings area.

steps.title: Create skeleton loading variants for major page surfaces
steps.description:
Decision 6, Part 1: Replace text-only loading states with skeleton loaders. The existing `Skeleton` component in `frontend/src/components/ui/Skeleton.tsx` is a simple animated div. Build composed skeleton variants: **Create `frontend/src/components/shared/skeletons/`:** 1. **`PlanHeaderSkeleton.tsx`**: Mimics the plan header Card layout ÔÇö badge placeholder, action button placeholders. 2. **`CommitListSkeleton.tsx`**: Shows 3-4 skeleton commit rows matching the `CommitList` SortableItem layout ÔÇö grip handle, title bar, chess piece dot, points, action buttons. 3. **`TeamWeekSkeleton.tsx`**: Overview card grid with skeleton stat boxes matching `OverviewSection`. 4. **`ReportChartSkeleton.tsx`**: Generic chart placeholder ÔÇö card with header skeleton + rectangular area for chart body. **Update routes to use skeletons:** - `MyWeek.tsx`: Replace `
Loading planÔÇª
` with `` + `` - `TeamWeek.tsx`: Replace `
Loading team dataÔÇª
` with `` - `Reports.tsx`: Replace `

Loading report dataÔÇª

` with grid of `` - `Rcdos.tsx`: Replace `LoadingÔÇª` text with a tree-like skeleton - `Tickets.tsx`: Replace loading state with table skeleton rows **Tier 2 ÔÇö AI loading states** (already partially implemented): - `InsightPanel` already has `InsightSkeletons` Ô£ô - `ManagerAiSummaryCard` already has `ManagerAiSummarySkeleton` Ô£ô - `AiLintPanel`: Update "Analyzing commit qualityÔÇª" to include shimmer animation **Tier 3 ÔÇö Timeout fallback:** Create `frontend/src/components/shared/LoadingWithTimeout.tsx`: - Shows skeleton for first N seconds - After timeout (default 5s), shows "Still loadingÔÇª [Retry]" with elapsed time - Takes `onRetry` callback prop

steps.title: Add empty state components for major surfaces
steps.description:
Decision 6, Part 2: Create rich empty states with description + CTA for each major surface.

Create `frontend/src/components/shared/EmptyState.tsx`:
```tsx
interface EmptyStateProps {
  icon?: ReactNode;         // Lucide icon or emoji illustration
  title: string;            // e.g., "No commits yet"
  description?: string;     // Explanatory text
  action?: ReactNode;       // CTA button
  'data-testid'?: string;
}
```

Styled consistently: centered content, muted text, subtle border, generous padding.

**Apply to surfaces:**

1. **MyWeek (no plan / empty commits)**: `planLoading` is false and `!plan` ÔåÆ show EmptyState with title "Start your week", description "Add your first commitment to begin planning", CTA ÔåÆ Add Commit button.

2. **CommitList (empty)**: When `commits.length === 0` and plan exists ÔåÆ EmptyState "No commits yet" with prominent Add button. Currently shows nothing.

3. **TeamWeek (no team selected)**: Already has a "No team selected" div ÔÇö enhance with EmptyState component for consistency.

4. **Reports (no data)**: When all report arrays are empty ÔåÆ EmptyState "Not enough data yet ÔÇö reports will populate after your first reconciled week".

5. **RCDO tree (empty)**: When `tree` is empty array ÔåÆ EmptyState "No strategy nodes ÔÇö create your first Rally Cry to get started" with CTA if user has permissions.

6. **Tickets (empty results)**: When ticket list returns 0 results ÔåÆ EmptyState "No tickets match your filters" or "No tickets yet ÔÇö create your first ticket".

Write unit test for EmptyState component.

steps.title: Install Tremor charting library and migrate velocity trend chart
steps.description:
Decision 5, Part 1: Add Tremor as a dependency and migrate the first (most complex) chart as proof of concept.

**Install:** `npm install @tremor/react` in the frontend package. Tremor is Tailwind-native and works with Tailwind v4.

**Migrate `VelocityTrendChart`** in `frontend/src/routes/Reports.tsx`:
- Replace the manual CSS bar chart + rolling average dots with Tremor's `AreaChart` or `BarChart` component
- Include the rolling 4-week average as a second series (line overlay)
- Preserve the trend indicator badge in the header
- Add proper tooltips (Tremor provides them out of the box)
- Ensure dark mode works (Tremor respects Tailwind dark mode)
- Keep the same Card wrapper and header layout

**Keep pure CSS for:**
- `CapacityMeter` progress bar (simple, works well)
- Chess distribution stacked bar (unique visual, Tremor doesn't have a great match)

Verify bundle size impact is acceptable. If Tremor has issues with Tailwind v4 compatibility, fall back to Recharts (`npm install recharts`) which has no CSS framework dependency.

Update any snapshot or assertion tests for Reports if they exist.

steps.title: Migrate remaining charts to charting library
steps.description:
Decision 5, Part 2: Migrate the remaining CSS charts to the charting library chosen in step-11.

**Charts to migrate:**

1. **PlannedVsAchievedChart**: Grouped bar chart (planned vs achieved per week). Use `BarChart` with two series.

2. **AchievementRateChart**: Bar chart with target line at 80%. Use `BarChart` with reference line.

3. **CarryForwardChart**: Horizontal bar chart showing carry-forward rates. Use `BarChart` horizontal or `BarList`.

4. **ComplianceChart**: Multi-series horizontal bars (lock rate, reconcile rate, auto-lock). Use grouped `BarChart`.

5. **ScopeChangeChart**: Vertical bars with HIGH annotation. Use `BarChart` with conditional coloring.

**Keep as CSS:**
- Chess distribution stacked bar (custom visual)
- Exception aging table (it's a table, not a chart)
- AI acceptance card bars (simple progress bars)

Each migrated chart should:
- Maintain the same Card wrapper and title
- Use consistent color tokens (integrate with our semantic color system where appropriate)
- Include proper ARIA labels and screen reader support
- Handle empty data states gracefully
- Support dark mode

This is an incremental migration ÔÇö each chart is a self-contained function component in Reports.tsx.
